title: dicliptera maculata subsp. hirta
intro: Dicliptera maculata subsp. hirta, also known as the hairy Brazilian psyllium or hairy psyllium, is a plant species that belongs to the family Acanthaceae. It is traditionally used for its medicinal properties, which include its ability to stimulate bowel movements, lower cholesterol levels, and help with weight management due to its high fiber content. The plant has been used in herbal preparations to treat digestive issues, such as constipation and irritable bowel syndrome, and has also been used to lower blood pressure and reduce inflammation. Herbal preparations of Dicliptera maculata subsp. hirta can be made from the dried roots, leaves, and stems, and are often consumed as a tea or capsule.
